perturbation.original_text: 1.3 C onfidential Information shall be identified either by marking it, in the case of written materials, or, in the case of information that is disclosed orally or written materials that are not marked, by notifying the other Party of the confiden tial nature of the information. Such notification shall be done orally, by e -mail or written correspondence, or via other appropriate means of communication.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 Confidential Information will be marked as such. The method of marking is at the discretion of the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies how confidential information should be identified (marking or notification). By stating that the method of marking is at the discretion of the disclosing party, but removing the requirement to notify for unmarked information, creates an omission leading to in-text contradiction. This affects cases where written materials are not marked, the process of marking such information has been removed from the contract.This creates an ambiguity regarding information disclosed orally or in unmarked written materials, as the receiving party may not be aware it is deemed confidential. This contradicts section 3.2 where it talks about the receiving party will inform their employees about the confidential quality of the information provided and will ensure that their agreement is obtained to keep it confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 The Receiving Party will not disclose and will keep confidential the information received, except to its em ployees, representatives or agents who need to have access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of carrying out their duties in connection with the permitted purposes specified in clause 2. The Receiving Party will inform them about the confide ntial quality of the information provided and will ensure that their agreement is obtained to keep it confidential on the same terms as set forth in this Agreement. Hence, the Receiving Party w ill be responsible for ensuring that the obligations of confidentiality and non -use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel representatives or agents.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that confidential information is identified either by marking it or notifying the other party. The changed text removes the notification requirement for unmarked written materials, stating only that the method of marking is at the disclosing party's discretion. This omission creates a contradiction with section 3.2, which requires the receiving party to inform its employees about the confidential quality of the information. If the information is not marked and the disclosing party doesn't notify, the receiving party may not be aware of its confidential nature, making compliance with 3.2 impossible. This is a strong contradiction because it directly affects the receiving party's ability to fulfill its confidentiality obligations as stated in section 3.2.

perturbation.original_text: 3.7 The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all copies (in whatever form reproduce d or stored), including all notes and derivatives of the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreemen t; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may request it to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.7 The Receiving Party shall return all Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement upon request by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the Receiving Party's obligation to also destroy copies upon completion or termination of dealings/agreement creates an in-text contradiction with Section 3.8 which refers to retaining documents as required to comply with mandatory law, which assumes the documents would have otherwise been destroyed. This creates an inconsistency with Section 4.1.1, which states that the duty to hold in confidence Confidential Information shall remain in effect indefinitely.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.8 Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as required to comply with mandatory law, provided that such Confidential Informat ion or copies thereof shall be subject to an indefinite confidentiality obligation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the obligation for the Receiving Party to destroy copies of Confidential Information upon completion or termination of the agreement, only requiring return upon request. This directly contradicts section 3.8, which assumes that documents would otherwise be destroyed and allows the Receiving Party to retain documents to comply with mandatory law, subject to indefinite confidentiality. Because the changed text no longer mandates destruction, it clashes with the assumption in 3.8 and introduces a contradiction by creating an inconsistency in the document's instructions regarding destruction of documents. The original text creates a process where all documents are intended for destruction, while the changed text creates a process where the documents aren't intended for destruction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Duration and Termination 4.1.1 This Agreement will enter into force on the date on which it is signed. It will remain in force until the ILDA -care project is finalised . Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party’s duty to hold in confidence Confidential Information that wa s disclosed during the term shall remain in effect indefinitely, save otherwise agreed.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Duration and Termination 4.1.1 This Agreement will enter into force on the date on which it is signed. It will remain in force until the ILDA-care project is finalized.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the sentence 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party’s duty to hold in confidence Confidential Information that was disclosed during the term shall remain in effect indefinitely, save otherwise agreed' creates a contradiction with section 3.8, where the text says that the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as required to comply with mandatory law, provided that such Confidential Information or copies thereof shall be subject to an indefinite confidentiality obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.8 Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as required to comply with mandatory law, provided that such Confidential Informat ion or copies thereof shall be subject to an indefinite confidentiality obligation.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the confidentiality obligations survive the termination of the agreement indefinitely. The changed text removes this provision. The contradicted text (3.8) states that Confidential Information shall be subject to an indefinite confidentiality obligation, regardless of the term of the agreement. By removing the survival clause in 4.1.1, the changed text creates a contradiction with 3.8 because section 4.1.1 no longer explicitly supports the indefinite confidentiality obligation described in 3.8, creating a direct conflict regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Applicable Law and Jurisdiction This Agreement shall be construed and interpreted by German law. All disputes between signed parties shall fall under the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts who have jurisdiction for the district in which the German Partner has its registered office. The German Partner’s registered offi ce is located in Fr iedrichstraß e 10, 70174 Stuttgart, Germany.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Applicable Law and Jurisdiction This Agreement shall be construed and interpreted by the law of the jurisdiction where the disclosing party resides. All disputes between signed parties shall be resolved through mediation.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the specific German law and jurisdiction with a general reference to the disclosing party's jurisdiction and mandating mediation, an in-text contradiction is created with the signature sections. The original agreement specifies that BioRegio STERN Management GmbH, a German entity, has its registered office in Stuttgart, implying German jurisdiction. The modified clause creates confusion about which jurisdiction applies and contradicts the implicit agreement to German jurisdiction inherent in the signature block for the German partner.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non -Disclosure Agreement 11 BioRegio STERN Management GmbH Place and date:…………………………………………………………………………………………………………………..... Name and function of the signatory: …………………………… ………………………………………………………………………………………………… Signature/Stamp:… …..……………………………………………………………………………………………………………
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the specific German law and jurisdiction with a general reference to the disclosing party's jurisdiction and mandates mediation. The contradicted text contains the signature block for BioRegio STERN Management GmbH, a German entity with a registered office in Stuttgart, implicitly agreeing to German jurisdiction. The modified clause creates confusion about which jurisdiction applies and directly contradicts the implicit agreement to German jurisdiction inherent in the signature block for the German partner by specifying mediation instead of court jurisdiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Competition The receipt of Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement will not prevent or in any way limit either Party from developing, making or marketing products or services that are or may be competitive with the products or services of the other; or providing products or services to others who compete with the other Party; as long as those results have not become from a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Competition The Parties agree to refrain from engaging in activities that directly compete with the ILDA-care project during the term of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the competition clause to restrict competitive activities, it contradicts the Receiving Party’s rights and obligations as defined in section 3.3. It creates a contradiction where the agreement states that the receiving party will use the Confidential Information exclusively for the permitted purpose stated in clause 2 and not use the information for its own purposes or benefit. However, the competition clause now limits what it can do with its own independent projects if it directly competes with the ILDA-care project.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 The Receiving Par ty will use the Confidential Information exclusively for the permitted purpose stated in clause 2 and not use the information for its own purposes or benefit.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5, which now restricts competitive activities, directly contradicts Section 3.3. Section 3.3 states that the Receiving Party will use the Confidential Information exclusively for the permitted purpose and not for its own benefit. However, the new competition clause in Section 5 limits what the Receiving Party can do with its own independent projects if it directly competes with the ILDA-care project. This creates a conflict, as the Receiving Party's ability to use information or pursue projects for its own benefit is now restricted, while Section 3.3 implies it should not be.
